title: Unit 2: Searching
description: Learn how to find indices of items in arrays, including linear scanning and fast O(log n) boundary cuts.

topics.title: 2.1 Linear / Sequential Search
topics.description: Traverse the array from left to right, comparing each element with the target. Useful for unsorted data.

ispo.desc: Receives search array 'arr' and target value.
Allocates index tracking variable 'i'.
Iterates through array, checking if element matches target.
Returns index where element matches.
Returns -1 if target is not in the array.

quiz.q: What is the worst-case number of comparisons in a Linear Search on an array of size N?

topics.title: 2.2 Binary Search
topics.description: Finds targets in a SORTED array by dividing the search interval in half. Fast logarithmic runtime.

ispo.desc: Takes sorted array 'arr' and target value.
Allocates boundary markers low and high in storage memory.
Declares and calculates mid to store the middle index.
Checks middle value and halves the search window by shifting high or low pointers.
Returns index where element matches.
Returns -1 if boundaries cross without matching target.

quiz.q: What is the critical prerequisite for Binary Search?
o: The array size must be even
The array elements must be sorted
The array must not contain duplicate keys

topics.title: 2.3 Uniform Binary Search
topics.description: An optimization of binary search where a lookup table of midpoints is precomputed to avoid arithmetic division inside search loop.

ispo.desc: Accepts sorted array, target key, and precalculated offset array delta.
Creates pointer i, delta step counter d, and array limit parameter n.
Steps through loop. Checks index bounds. Increments or decrements index i using precalculated delta lookup values based on comparisons.
Returns -1 if pointer traverses outside array bounds.
Returns index i when element matches.
Returns -1 if loop breaks without a match.

quiz.q: What is the primary advantage of Uniform Binary Search over standard Binary Search?
o: Faster execution on systems where addition/subtraction is faster than division/shift operations

topics.title: 2.4 Interpolation Search
topics.description: An improvement over Binary Search for sorted arrays with uniformly distributed values. Uses linear interpolation formula to probe closer to target position.

complexity.time: O(log log n) average, O(n) worst case

topics.cheatSheet:
// Probing position calculation formula
pos = low + (((double)(high - low) / (arr[high] - arr[low])) * (target - arr[low]));

ispo.desc: Accepts sorted array and target value.
Allocates boundary variables low and high.
Calculates and stores position probe index 'pos'.
Calculates probe index using value ratios, evaluates matches, and shrinks search boundaries.
Handles single element range boundary checks and returns index or -1.
Returns matched probe position index.
Returns -1 if boundaries cross or target is outside array limits.

quiz.q: Under what distribution does Interpolation Search achieve its O(log log n) average complexity?

topics.title: 2.5 Fibonacci Search
topics.description: A divide and conquer searching method that uses Fibonacci numbers to divide the search interval. Uses addition and subtraction instead of division.

ispo.desc: Accepts sorted array and target key.
Stores size of array n.
Creates state variables for m, m-1, and m-2 Fibonacci offsets.
Creates offset pointer variable initialized to -1.
Calculates probe index variable i using offset and Fibonacci values.
Generates smallest Fibonacci number greater than or equal to n.
Loops. Adjusts Fibonacci variables to segment array, shifting offset when target exceeds arr[i].
Returns index i when element matches.
Evaluates final index element and returns offset + 1 if matching target.
Returns -1 if target is not found.

quiz.q: What mathematical properties does Fibonacci Search exploit to partition array bounds?
